name: Julio-Claudian Dynasty
description: The first imperial dynasty of Rome, descended from the Julian and Claudian families. Its five emperors — Augustus, Tiberius, Caligula, Claudius, and Nero — ruled from 27 BC to 68 AD, establishing the imperial system.

name: Flavian Dynasty
description: The second imperial dynasty, founded by Vespasian after the Year of the Four Emperors. The Flavians — Vespasian, Titus, and Domitian — ruled from 69 to 96 AD and built the Colosseum.

name: Nerva-Antonine Dynasty
description: The dynasty of the 'Five Good Emperors' — Nerva, Trajan, Hadrian, Antoninus Pius, and Marcus Aurelius — who ruled through adoption rather than hereditary succession. This period (96-192 AD) is considered the height of the Roman Empire.

name: Severan Dynasty
description: Imperial dynasty founded by Septimius Severus, the first emperor of African origin. The Severans (193-235 AD) expanded imperial power and military spending but faced increasing instability and succession crises.
